title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot discusses the murder of Joyce Reynolds with the family doctor, who was Joyce's physician. The doctor explains that Joyce was a healthy child who suffered occasionally from biliary disease but had no unusual medical history. When Poirot suggests that Joyce might have been killed because she "said too much," the doctor notes that while a witness's testimony can be a motive, the killer's motive might simply lie in a disturbed or evil mind. The doctor posits that the killer was likely present at the Halloween party, potentially as a guest, a servant, or someone who entered through a window after checking the latches.

Poirot then visits the Elm Tree School to meet Miss Emery. He explains that he is investigating the murder at the invitation of Mrs. Ariadne Oliver. Miss Emery describes Joyce as an ordinary child with a compulsive habit of lying to attract attention and boast to her friends. She suggests that Joyce’s claim of witnessing a murder earlier that day was likely an exaggeration of a mundane event, such as a car accident, intended to impress others or Mrs. Oliver. Miss Emery notes that most children at the party were between nine and fifteen years old and associated with the school. She also mentions a previous unsolved crime at the school: the strangulation of a melancholy twenty-four-year-old teacher named Miss White, which occurred a year or two prior.
